title: aspidium filix-mas var. lacerum
intro: Aspidium filix-mas var. lacerum, also known as the Lacerate Lady Fern, is a variety of the common Lady Fern (Aspidium filix-mas), a species of fern that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic qualities. The health benefits of Aspidium filix-mas var. lacerum include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin as a poultice or salve.
